instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Index-linked Insurance Plan: A New Way to Secure Your Future in 2025
Planning for the future is important. Everyone wants to keep their money safe and also see it grow gradually over time. In this regard, a new insurance policy deserves special mention. It is called an index-linked insurance policy. This plan is unique as it follows the ups and downs of popular market indexes like the Nifty or the Sensex. It also gives life cover, so your family stays protected.
This blog will help you understand what index-linked insurance is, how it works, why it is useful, and how it is different from other plans like ULIP.
Top Investment Plans 2025
If you are looking for new ways to keep your money safe and grow it slowly over time, an index-linked insurance policy may be a good option in 2025. It is reintroduced of index-linked product in the market that connects your returns to popular market indexes like Nifty or Sensex. This plan does not work like regular investment tools. Instead, it offers life cover and links savings to a market index, which decides how your money grows.
Many people prefer to buy index-linked life insurance because it can give better returns than traditional policies and is safer than investing fully in stocks. Plus, these plans are designed to follow IRDAI rules strictly. In this blog, we will explain what index-linked insurance is, how it works, who manages it, and how it is different from ULIP.
What is an Index-linked Insurance Policy?
An index-linked insurance policy is a life insurance plan that helps you grow your savings while keeping you protected. In this plan, your money is linked to a market index like Nifty, Sensex, or government bonds. If these indexes go up, your savings also grow. If the indexes fall, your returns may be lower, but you usually won’t lose money. Along with savings, it also gives you life cover. This means your family will get money if something happens to you. It is different from fixed-return plans because the returns change with the market index. It is also different from ULIPs, which allow you to pick from different types of funds. Here, the money goes to one chosen index. This plan is simple and clear and helps people who want both savings and protection over the long term.
Why Index-linked Insurance Plan?
There are many reasons why an index-linked insurance plan can be useful. It gives you a mix of life cover and savings that grow with the market. Below are the key points that make this plan helpful:
Safer Option: The plan is linked to well-known indexes like government bonds or Nifty, which are safer compared to full equity funds.
No Need to Choose Funds: Unlike ULIPs, you don’t need to pick from different funds.
Low Charges: Since no active fund switching is needed, charges stay low.
Simple Structure: It’s easy to understand and track your returns.
IRDAI Approved: It follows all rules set by the regulator, making it a trusted product.
This plan is suitable for those who want steady growth without dealing with complicated investment choices.
What are the Duties of the Working Group?
The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) has formed a special group to manage and check everything about index-linked insurance plans. This group is called the working group. Here are their main jobs:
Find out if there is a real need for index-linked insurance in India.
Check which index-linked products are already being sold.
Learn how other countries handle similar plans.
Decide how the plan should be priced and designed.
Ask outside experts for help if needed.
Make sure the plan follows all safety and return rules.
Send a full report to IRDAI within two months of the order date.
This group is essential because it helps create a plan that is safe, simple, and useful for people who want both savings and protection linked to market performance.
What is the Structure of the Index-linked Insurance Plan?
This plan will have a lock-in period of five years. It will work like a regular premium plan or single premium plan, depending on your choice. One of the key points is that it will not be bundled with any other products. That means it will not come as a gift with a loan, ticket, or card. It will be sold alone.
This clear structure makes the plan simple and easy to understand for buyers. It also follows all IRDAI rules, which increases trust in the plan.
The Cost Structure and the Returns
Here’s how the charges and returns work:
Mortality Charges: These will be clearly shown and deducted. This is the cost for life cover.
Surrender or Withdrawal Charges: These may be deducted if the policy is closed early. These are spread throughout the term.
Benchmark Index: The returns will follow an index approved by IRDAI.
This means policyholders get a clear idea of what they are paying and how their returns are calculated.
How Will an Index-Linked Insurance Policy Benefit?
It offers protection and planned returns together.
It follows a safe route by linking to indexes.
Fund managers do not need to actively manage the money.
The charges are low and simple.
It helps you build long-term savings without confusion.
How Will an Index-linked Insurance Plan Operate?
The policy works like a bank account. You pay premiums.
The policy has a managed account linked to a benchmark index.
At the end of each reporting period, you get a statement.
The account shows paid premiums + interest based on the index.
The death benefit will be 10 times the life cover or sum assured.
This makes it very easy for the policyholder to track their savings.
What Are Surrender Charges?
If the policy is stopped in the first 3 years and not revived, the surrender value will be:
Policy account value at the end of the 5-year lock-in
Minus surrender charges
If stopped after 3 years, the surrender value is:
Account value at the date of surrender or lock-in end (whichever is later)
Minus surrender charges
Is an ILIP Different From ULIP?
Yes, ILIP is different from ULIP:
ULIP allows policyholders to choose funds like equity, hybrid, debt, etc.
ILIP follows a fixed index (like the Sensex).
ULIP returns change based on fund choice.
ILIP gives non-negative returns mandate for pension-related ILIPs, this is not guaranteed for all ILIPs.
This makes ILIP simpler and more stable than ULIP.
FAQs
What is an index-linked insurance policy?Index-linked insurance plans are a type of life insurance policy in which the value of your investment grows based on a stock market index, such as Nifty or Sensex. If the market index goes up, then the value of your insurance policy grows. If the market drops, your returns may also be lower. This is coupled with life cover, meaning upon your demise your family will receive the notional amount at the time of your death. This type of plan combines, a life cover policy with growth potential.
What is an IUL insurance policy? IUL stands for Indexed Universal Life insurance. It is a type of life insurance plan where your money grows based on the market index, but with limits. Even if the market performs badly, you won’t lose all your money. It also lets you change your premium payments and life cover amount. So, it gives you life protection plus steady growth linked to market performance, but with more safety and flexibility than other plans.
Who should buy IUL insurance? People who want both life insurance and slow, steady money growth can think about buying IUL insurance. It is good for those who do not want to take big risks but still want to grow their savings over time. It is helpful for long-term planning, like saving for retirement or your children’s future, while staying protected with life cover. If you prefer safety with some market benefit, IUL can work well for you.
What is an example of index linking? Let’s say your insurance policy is linked to the Sensex index. If the Sensex grows by 10% in a year, your policy money also grows by nearly 10%. This means your savings increase based on how well the market index performs. This is called index linking—your return follows the index. It allows your money to grow when the market does well, but also comes with some protection when the market doesn’t perform strongly.
Conclusion
The index linked insurance policy is a fresh idea in the Indian life insurance space. It is designed for people who want the safety of traditional insurance and the growth potential of market indexes. IRDAI is making sure the plan is safe, transparent, and useful for policyholders.
With growing interest in passive products, an index linked life insurance plan may find a strong place in 2025 and beyond. It gives a balance of safety, returns, and life cover, especially when used for long-term savings or pension needs. Before choosing any plan, make sure to understand your needs, read all details, and stay updated with IRDAI changes.
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Ltd., Regd. office Address: Bajaj Allianz House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006, Reg. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in
